name: Architecture anti-pattern
description: An architecture anti-pattern is a common but ineffective solution to a recurring design problem in software development, often leading to poor system structure and maintainability. Examples include "Big Ball of Mud," where a system lacks a clear architecture, and "Blind Pattern Following," where developers adopt practices without understanding their purpose.

name: Vulnerability
description: A software vulnerability is a flaw or weakness in software code that can be exploited by attackers to compromise the security and functionality of a system. These vulnerabilities can arise from coding errors, design oversights, or outdated software, posing risks to data and systems.

name: Configuration Error
description: A software configuration error is a mistake in the system settings that can lead to incorrect operations or system failures. Common causes include human error, system malfunctions, and issues during data integration.

name: Other
description: Issues that cannot be classified into other categories.

name: Threat Vector
description: A potential attack path or method that could be used by threat actors to compromise system security, identified through threat modeling analysis.

name: Missing Security Control
description: An absent or insufficient security control that should be implemented to mitigate identified threats and reduce system risk.
